headline: Jim Cramer Warns Of 'Sloppy' US Open As South Korea's KOSPI Crashes 12%
summary: Jim Cramer flagged risk of a sloppy U.S. open Wednesday as South Korea's KOSPI crashed 12%, with memory-stock traders reportedly selling U.S. equities to raise cash. Futures, however, pointed higher.

headline: Western Digital Refines Capital Structure And Brand Focus After Spin Off
summary: Western Digital fully redeemed its senior notes, reshaping its capital structure shortly after the spin off of its flash memory business. The company relaunched and unified its professional storage line under the G-DRIVE brand, refining its identity for content creators. These moves align with Western Digital’s focus on operating as a standalone HDD business with a clearer product offering and balance sheet. Western Digital (NasdaqGS: WDC) is drawing fresh attention as it reshapes both its...
